results.query: What is the latest security notice email about Passkeys in my inbox?
results.ground_truth_answer: You received an email from Microsoft (microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com) titled "Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication" received on July 22, 2026.
results.app_answer:
The latest security notice email in your inbox regarding Passkeys is:

### 🔗 [Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication](https://outlook.office365.com/owa/?ItemID=AAMkADZiYjQ4MmY0LTdiMDgtNGRhOS1iY2I4LWMyNmFmZTAzMjRjNQBGAAAAAADDgpxR%2FAXhSbXifCNkUZqHBwBxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAAAAAAEMAABxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAACl5DmRAAA%3D&exvsurl=1&viewmodel=ReadMessageItem)
* **Sender:** Microsoft (`microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com`)
* **Received:** Wednesday, July 22, 2026 at 7:05 AM EDT
* **Status:** Unread

---

### **Key Summary & Timeline**
The email details Microsoft’s transition to stronger, phishing-resistant authentication in Microsoft Entra ID by phasing out traditional SMS and voice MFA in favor of **Passkeys**. 

* **The Reason:** SMS and voice options are highly vulnerable to phishing, SIM-swapping, and replay attacks. Passkeys will now serve as the default.
* **Key Dates to Note:**
  * **September 1, 2026**: Users currently relying on SMS or voice authentication will be automatically enabled for passkeys and nudged to register one during their next multi-factor authentication (MFA) prompt. 
  * **February 1, 2027**: Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired. After this date, users who have only SMS or voice set up will receive a blocking prompt and must register a passkey to proceed with sign-in.

---

### **Action Items for Administrators**
If your tenant currently has users utilizing SMS or voice, Microsoft recommends taking the following steps before the enforcement deadlines:
1. **Identify Affected Users**: Find who is still using SMS or voice for authentication.
2. **Begin Transition Campaign**: Enable passkeys and drive user enrollment before the automatic enablement on September 1, 2026.
3. **Communicate**: Inform your users of the upcoming changes and migration schedules.
4. **Alternative Options**: If you have a regulatory requirement to keep SMS/voice, you must configure a customer-managed telecom provider via the Microsoft Security Store (available starting October 30, 2026).

results.query: What action is recommended regarding Azure Copilot agent access settings?
results.ground_truth_answer: An email from Microsoft Azure (azure-noreply@microsoft.com) recommends reviewing Azure Copilot agent access settings before 1 August 2026.
results.app_answer:
Based on the notification received from Microsoft Azure, starting **August 1, 2026**, Azure Copilot is transitioning from the existing "Agent mode" experience to direct access to individual agents. By default, all Azure Copilot agents (including those in Public Preview) will be enabled for tenants where Azure Copilot is already enabled.

### **Recommended Actions**

Depending on your organization's preferences:

*   **To keep all Azure Copilot agents enabled**: 
    *   **No action is required**.
*   **To prevent agents from being enabled by default**:
    *   **Before August 1, 2026**: Disable Azure Copilot at the tenant level. (See the [Manage access to Azure Copilot documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/copilot/manage-access) for instructions).
    *   **On or after August 1, 2026**: Review and configure individual agent access settings in the **Azure Copilot Admin Center** to enable or disable specific agents (such as the *Observability*, *Deployment*, *Troubleshooting*, *Optimization*, *Resiliency*, or *Migration* agents).

---

**Source Email**: 🔗 [Action recommended: Review Azure Copilot agent access settings before 1 August 2026](https://outlook.office365.com/owa/?ItemID=AAMkADZiYjQ4MmY0LTdiMDgtNGRhOS1iY2I4LWMyNmFmZTAzMjRjNQBGAAAAAADDgpxR%2FAXhSbXifCNkUZqHBwBxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAAAAAAEMAABxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAACl5DmPAAA%3D&exvsurl=1&viewmodel=ReadMessageItem)

results.app_answer:
I found **two (2) recent Microsoft system notifications** received today, **Wednesday, July 22, 2026**, along with a notable notification from yesterday, **July 21, 2026**.

Here is a summary of these notifications, including actionable steps and links to view them directly in your Outlook Web App:

---

### 1. 🔐 Security Notification: Passkeys by Default & SMS/Voice Retirement
* **Received**: July 22, 2026, at 7:05 AM EDT
* **Sender**: Microsoft (`microsoft-noreply@microsoft.com`)
* **Source Email Link**: 🔗 [Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication](https://outlook.office365.com/owa/?ItemID=AAMkADZiYjQ4MmY0LTdiMDgtNGRhOS1iY2I4LWMyNmFmZTAzMjRjNQBGAAAAAADDgpxR%2FAXhSbXifCNkUZqHBwBxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAAAAAAEMAABxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAACl5DmRAAA%3D&exvsurl=1&viewmodel=ReadMessageItem)
* **Key Details & Deadlines**:
  * **What's Changing**: Microsoft is moving all Microsoft Entra ID tenants to phishing-resistant authentication. **Passkeys are becoming the default** experience. Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication will be fully retired.
  * **September 1, 2026**: Users currently using SMS or voice will be automatically enabled for passkeys and nudged to register one on their next Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) sign-in.
  * **February 1, 2027**: Microsoft-provided SMS and voice are retired. Anyone who has not migrated to passkeys (or another phishing-resistant method) will face a blocking prompt upon signing in.
  * **Action Items**: 
    1. Identify all users in your tenant currently enabled for SMS or voice.
    2. Drive adoption of passkeys before the September 1st auto-enablement.
    3. Evaluate/configure custom telecom providers in the Microsoft Security Store before Feb 1, 2027, if keeping SMS/voice is an absolute regulatory requirement.

---

### 2. ⚠️ System Delivery Report (NDR): Undeliverable Email
* **Received**: July 22, 2026, at 8:48 AM EDT
* **Sender**: Microsoft Outlook (`MicrosoftExchange329e71ec88ae4615bbc36ab6ce41109e@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com`)
* **Source Email Link**: 🔗 [Undeliverable: Q4 Resource Allocation](https://outlook.office365.com/owa/?ItemID=AAMkADZiYjQ4MmY0LTdiMDgtNGRhOS1iY2I4LWMyNmFmZTAzMjRjNQBGAAAAAADDgpxR%2FAXhSbXifCNkUZqHBwBxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAAAAAAEMAABxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAACl5DmSAAA%3D&exvsurl=1&viewmodel=ReadMessageItem)
* **Key Details**: 
  * An email sent by Jesus Chavez (`admin@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com`) with the subject **"Q4 Resource Allocation"** could not be delivered to `sarah.jenkins@sockcop.onmicrosoft.com`.
  * **Reason**: SMTP address lookup failed (Error: `550 5.1.10 RecipientNotFound`). The recipient `sarah.jenkins` was not found at `sockcop.onmicrosoft.com`.

---

### 3. ☁️ Service Action Recommended: Azure Copilot Settings (Recent from Yesterday)
* **Received**: July 21, 2026, at 8:30 PM EDT
* **Sender**: Microsoft Azure (`azure-noreply@microsoft.com`)
* **Source Email Link**: 🔗 [Action recommended: Review Azure Copilot agent access settings before 1 August 2026](https://outlook.office365.com/owa/?ItemID=AAMkADZiYjQ4MmY0LTdiMDgtNGRhOS1iY2I4LWMyNmFmZTAzMjRjNQBGAAAAAADDgpxR%2FAXhSbXifCNkUZqHBwBxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAAAAAAEMAABxRvw3GvQtRYuHRCU20sFzAACl5DmPAAA%3D&exvsurl=1&viewmodel=ReadMessageItem)
